evidence: First meeting between V and Chris, connected through V's executive function coach Heather who works with Chris's organization.

source_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: V: 'I've always been so interested in building out stuff that, you know, essentially tries to act as a, like, crutch. Like a, like, essentially the equivalent of like a brain crutch for someone with ADHD.'

source_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: Chris is building 360 product for ADHD support: 'filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching'
V warned against 'death by a thousand cuts' of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes and identify design partners willing to work with the product as-is.
V emphasized that founder-led sales provides customer research, network building, and messaging refinement—critical activities for early-stage companies.
V defined product-market fit using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: 'you know it when you see it'—when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly.

Chris: 'My drive for authenticity is part of the reason why I don't go on social media so much is just like. Feels like it's all. Feels like I'd have to be disingenuous.' V countered that social media only works long-term when done authentically.
Chris revealed he's been building his edtech product for almost a year, targeting organizations, school districts, and clinics. Pricing: $10/month individuals, $20/month supporters.
Vrijen invited Chris to join the Future of Higher Education group (6,000 members globally, ~1,000 in NYC, ~1,000 in Boston), where Vrijen serves as NYC co-lead.
V: 'We are a great solution because we collect first-party data.' Positioned Careerspan as 'human data company' rather than AI company or recruiting tool.
V: 'This is opted in candidate data. As things heat up with publicly scraped data, like this is a really valuable strategy for you guys.' Referenced Eightfold lawsuit as proof point.
Logan's insight: 'Why don't we make a 30-second look at the information we capture via Careerspan?' Shift from explaining to showing—visual demonstration over word-heavy explanations.
V: 'Sourcing and matching become the same thing.' Once you have a scannable talent pipeline, the distinction between finding candidates and assessing fit collapses.
V: 'Internal talent development and talent identification is the new edge.' The best hire is who you already have.
V: 'With Careerspan, you're building a synthetic talent pipeline that you can scan at any moment.' Framing value as ownable, scannable talent asset rather than just a database.
V and Logan conducted 'collateral blitz' session—clear division of labor: V handles demo video/script, Logan handles email content and prospect identification.
V: 'Donna and Donatella are the same person. One is with a story, one is with stories, one is without.' Illustrates how much more depth Careerspan captures through narrative context.
